name: cep_exists_flush_transient
drl: rule R when exists E1() P() then end
_finding: D-152: a DUE-ON-ARRIVAL witness (nonneg deadline <= clock) still satisfies at its exec — the re-fire batch FIRES (the agenda drains before flushExpirations) and the witness drops at quiescence. P2 then E1(ts=10, deadline 111 <= clock 300): drain [P2] -> rtm=[P1,P2]; satisfy at exec: reverse = [2,1]; quiescence retracts E1. Cycle fires [2,1] transiently; final WM has no E1. The engine pre-D-152 LEAKED the witness (D-133's boundary conflated nonneg-past with negative deadlines).
